---
title: "Anexo: Figuras complementarias"
subtitle: "Transcriptomic Analysis of Murine Liver Under Microgravity Conditions Using RNA-seq"
author: "Zyanya Valentina Velazquez Aldrete"
date: "31 de mayo de 2026"
output:
  pdf_document:
    toc: false
    number_sections: true
    latex_engine: xelatex
fontsize: 12pt
header-includes:
  - \usepackage{float}
  - \floatplacement{figure}{H}
---

# Anexo: Figuras complementarias

## A.1 Control de calidad - MultiQC

Las siguientes figuras muestran los reportes completos de MultiQC antes y después del filtrado con fastp.

```{r fig-multiqc-raw, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="A1.Resumen MultiQC de las lecturas crudas (antes del filtrado).", out.width="100%", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics("/export/space3/users/zyanyava/2026-2/Transcriptomica/RNA_seq_Project/docs/figures/Multiqc_raw.png")
```

```{r fig-multiqc-clean, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="A2.Resumen MultiQC de las lecturas despues del filtrado.", out.width="100%", fig.align="center"}
knitr::include_graphics("/export/space3/users/zyanyava/2026-2/Transcriptomica/RNA_seq_Project/docs/figures/multiqc_clean.png")
```

## A.2 Comparación de pipelines de alineamiento

Se evaluaron tres pipelines de alineamiento y cuantificación: STAR + featureCounts, HISAT2 + featureCounts y Salmon (pseudoalineamiento). La Tabla A1 muestra las métricas de desempeño para cada pipeline.

```{r tabla-pipelines, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

# Datos reales obtenidos de los logs de ejecución
tabla_pipelines <- data.frame(
  Pipeline = c("STAR + featureCounts", "HISAT2 + featureCounts", "Salmon"),
  `Mapeo (%)` = c(87.57, 96.62, 84.98),
  `Tiempo (min)` = c("4.97", "7.25", "2.20"),
  `Memoria (GB)` = c("27.9", "4.0", "3.8"),
  `Detección de splicing` = c("Sí", "Sí", "No"),
  `Conserva info genómica` = c("Sí", "Sí", "No"),
  `Selección` = c("**Principal**", "Descartado", "Validación")
)

kable(tabla_pipelines, 
      caption = "Tabla A2: Comparación de pipelines de alineamiento y cuantificación.",
      booktabs = TRUE,
      align = "lcccccll") %>%
  kable_styling(latex_options = c("hold_position", "scale_down"), font_size = 8)
```


### Justificación de la selección del pipeline

Se evaluaron tres pipelines de alineamiento y cuantificación. HISAT2 + featureCounts presentó el mayor porcentaje de alineamiento (96.62%), seguido por STAR (87.57%) y Salmon (84.98%). En cuanto al tiempo de ejecución, Salmon fue el más rápido (2.20 min), seguido por STAR (4.97 min) y HISAT2 (7.25 min).

STAR + featureCounts fue seleccionado como pipeline principal por las siguientes razones prácticas:

1. Integración con el flujo de trabajo: El pipeline en Nextflow estaba diseñado originalmente para STAR, y modificarlo para HISAT2 requería cambios extensos que no pudieron realizarse dentro del tiempo del proyecto.
2. Estándar en la literatura: STAR es el alineador más utilizado en estudios de RNA-seq, lo que facilita la comparación con otros trabajos.
3. Formato de logs: STAR genera archivos _Log.final.out más estructurados para extraer métricas de calidad.
4. Detección de splicing: Ambos métodos (STAR y HISAT2) conservan información de splicing, a diferencia de Salmon.

Salmon se mantuvo como estrategia de validación por su uso en el artículo original de Kurosawa et al. y por ser el método de cuantificación más rápido. Su falta de conservación de información genómica lo hace menos adecuado como pipeline principal para este estudio.

Por lo tanto, todos los análisis de expresión diferencial y visualizaciones presentadas en el cuerpo principal del proyecto se realizaron con el pipeline STAR + featureCounts.

## A.3 Análisis de componentes principales (PCA)

PCA de los perfiles transcriptómicos para los pipelines STAR y Salmon.

```{r fig-pca-star, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="A3.PCA de muestras (STAR/featureCounts).", out.width="60%", fig.align="center", warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
knitr::include_graphics("/export/space3/users/zyanyava/2026-2/Transcriptomica/RNA_seq_Project/results/deseq2/plots/PCA_plot_groups.png")
```

```{r fig-pca-salmon, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="A4.PCA de muestras (Salmon/tximport).", out.width="60%", fig.align="center", warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
knitr::include_graphics("/export/space3/users/zyanyava/2026-2/Transcriptomica/RNA_seq_Project/results/deseq2_salmon/plots/PCA_plot_groups.png")
```

## A.4 Volcano plots completos

**Volcano plots para los contrastes MG vs GC y A1G vs GC en STAR**

```{r fig-volcano-mg-full, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="A5: Volcano plot - MG vs GC (STAR).", out.width="60%", fig.align="center", warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

res_mg <- read.csv("/export/space3/users/zyanyava/2026-2/Transcriptomica/RNA_seq_Project/results/deseq2/tables/MG_vs_GC.csv")

res_mg <- res_mg %>%
  mutate(regulation = case_when(
    padj < 0.05 & log2FoldChange > 1 ~ "Upregulated",
    padj < 0.05 & log2FoldChange < -1 ~ "Downregulated",
    TRUE ~ "Not significant"
  ))

n_up <- sum(res_mg$regulation == "Upregulated")
n_down <- sum(res_mg$regulation == "Downregulated")

ggplot(res_mg, aes(x = log2FoldChange, y = -log10(padj), color = regulation)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Upregulated" = "red", "Downregulated" = "blue", "Not significant" = "gray")) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-1, 1), linetype = "dashed", color = "black", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dashed", color = "black", alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(title = "MG vs GC",
       x = "log2 Fold Change",
       y = "-log10(padj)",
       subtitle = paste0("Upregulated: ", n_up, " | Downregulated: ", n_down)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

```{r fig-volcano-a1g-full, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="Figura A6: Volcano plot - A1G vs GC (STAR).", out.width="60%", fig.align="center", warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

res_a1g <- read.csv("/export/space3/users/zyanyava/2026-2/Transcriptomica/RNA_seq_Project/results/deseq2/tables/A1G_vs_GC.csv")

res_a1g <- res_a1g %>%
  mutate(regulation = case_when(
    padj < 0.05 & log2FoldChange > 1 ~ "Upregulated",
    padj < 0.05 & log2FoldChange < -1 ~ "Downregulated",
    TRUE ~ "Not significant"
  ))

n_up <- sum(res_a1g$regulation == "Upregulated")
n_down <- sum(res_a1g$regulation == "Downregulated")

ggplot(res_a1g, aes(x = log2FoldChange, y = -log10(padj), color = regulation)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Upregulated" = "red", "Downregulated" = "blue", "Not significant" = "gray")) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-1, 1), linetype = "dashed", color = "black", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dashed", color = "black", alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(title = "A1G vs GC",
       x = "log2 Fold Change",
       y = "-log10(padj)",
       subtitle = paste0("Upregulated: ", n_up, " | Downregulated: ", n_down)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

**Volcano plots para los contrastes MG vs GC y A1G vs GC en Salmon**

```{r fig-volcano-mg-salmon, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="A7: Volcano plot - MG vs GC (Salmon).", out.width="60%", fig.align="center", warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

res_mg_salmon <- read.csv("/export/space3/users/zyanyava/2026-2/Transcriptomica/RNA_seq_Project/results/deseq2_salmon/tables/MG_vs_GC.csv")

res_mg_salmon <- res_mg_salmon %>%
  mutate(regulation = case_when(
    padj < 0.05 & log2FoldChange > 1 ~ "Upregulated",
    padj < 0.05 & log2FoldChange < -1 ~ "Downregulated",
    TRUE ~ "Not significant"
  ))

n_up <- sum(res_mg_salmon$regulation == "Upregulated")
n_down <- sum(res_mg_salmon$regulation == "Downregulated")

ggplot(res_mg_salmon, aes(x = log2FoldChange, y = -log10(padj), color = regulation)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Upregulated" = "red", "Downregulated" = "blue", "Not significant" = "gray")) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-1, 1), linetype = "dashed", color = "black", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dashed", color = "black", alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(title = "MG vs GC (Salmon)",
       x = "log2 Fold Change",
       y = "-log10(padj)",
       subtitle = paste0("Upregulated: ", n_up, " | Downregulated: ", n_down)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

```{r fig-volcano-a1g-salmon, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="Figura A8: Volcano plot - A1G vs GC (Salmon).", out.width="60%", fig.align="center",  warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

res_a1g_salmon <- read.csv("/export/space3/users/zyanyava/2026-2/Transcriptomica/RNA_seq_Project/results/deseq2_salmon/tables/A1G_vs_GC.csv")

res_a1g_salmon <- res_a1g_salmon %>%
  mutate(regulation = case_when(
    padj < 0.05 & log2FoldChange > 1 ~ "Upregulated",
    padj < 0.05 & log2FoldChange < -1 ~ "Downregulated",
    TRUE ~ "Not significant"
  ))

n_up <- sum(res_a1g_salmon$regulation == "Upregulated")
n_down <- sum(res_a1g_salmon$regulation == "Downregulated")

ggplot(res_a1g_salmon, aes(x = log2FoldChange, y = -log10(padj), color = regulation)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Upregulated" = "red", "Downregulated" = "blue", "Not significant" = "gray")) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(-1, 1), linetype = "dashed", color = "black", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dashed", color = "black", alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(title = "A1G vs GC (Salmon)",
       x = "log2 Fold Change",
       y = "-log10(padj)",
       subtitle = paste0("Upregulated: ", n_up, " | Downregulated: ", n_down)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```


## A.5 Heatmap de expresión

Heatmap con los top 30 genes diferencialmente expresados en STAR

```{r fig-heatmap-full, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="Figura A9: Heatmap de expresión (top 30 genes DE en MG vs GC).", out.width="80%", fig.align="center", warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
knitr::include_graphics("/export/space3/users/zyanyava/2026-2/Transcriptomica/RNA_seq_Project/results/deseq2/plots/heatmap_top30.png")
```

Heatmap con los top 30 genes diferencialmente expresados en Salmon

```{r fig-heatmap-salmon, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="Figura A10: Heatmap de expresión (top 30 genes DE en MG vs GC).", out.width="80%", fig.align="center", warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
knitr::include_graphics("/export/space3/users/zyanyava/2026-2/Transcriptomica/RNA_seq_Project/results/deseq2_salmon/plots/heatmap_top30.png")
```

## A.6 Scatter plots de expresión diferencial

### A.6.1 Scatter plot - STAR (MG vs GC vs A1G vs GC)

El siguiente scatter plot compara los log2 fold changes de los contrastes MG vs GC y A1G vs GC obtenidos con STAR/featureCounts.

```{r fig-scatter-star, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="A11: Comparación de log2FC entre MG vs GC y A1G vs GC (STAR).", out.width="60%", fig.align="center", message=FALSE}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Leer resultados de STAR
res_mg_star <- read.csv("/export/space3/users/zyanyava/2026-2/Transcriptomica/RNA_seq_Project/results/deseq2/tables/MG_vs_GC.csv")
res_a1g_star <- read.csv("/export/space3/users/zyanyava/2026-2/Transcriptomica/RNA_seq_Project/results/deseq2/tables/A1G_vs_GC.csv")

# Unir por gene_name
res_star <- merge(
  res_mg_star[, c("gene_name", "log2FoldChange", "padj")],
  res_a1g_star[, c("gene_name", "log2FoldChange", "padj")],
  by = "gene_name",
  suffixes = c("_MG", "_A1G")
)

# Calcular correlación
cor_star <- cor(res_star$log2FoldChange_MG, res_star$log2FoldChange_A1G, use = "complete.obs")

# Clasificar significancia
res_star <- res_star %>%
  mutate(significant = case_when(
    padj_MG < 0.05 & padj_A1G < 0.05 ~ "Both significant",
    padj_MG < 0.05 ~ "Only MG",
    padj_A1G < 0.05 ~ "Only A1G",
    TRUE ~ "Not significant"
  ))

# Scatter plot
ggplot(res_star, aes(x = log2FoldChange_MG, y = log2FoldChange_A1G, color = significant)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 0.8) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Both significant" = "red", 
                                "Only MG" = "blue", 
                                "Only A1G" = "green", 
                                "Not significant" = "gray")) +
  labs(x = "log2FC MG vs GC",
       y = "log2FC A1G vs GC",
       title = "STAR: MG vs GC vs A1G vs GC",
       subtitle = paste0("Pearson r = ", round(cor_star, 3))) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

### A.6.2 Scatter plot - Salmon (MG vs GC vs A1G vs GC)

El siguiente scatter plot compara los log2 fold changes de los contrastes MG vs GC y A1G vs GC obtenidos con Salmon/tximport.

```{r fig-scatter-salmon, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="A12: Comparación de log2FC entre MG vs GC y A1G vs GC (Salmon).", out.width="60%", fig.align="center", message=FALSE}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Leer resultados de Salmon
res_mg_salmon <- read.csv("/export/space3/users/zyanyava/2026-2/Transcriptomica/RNA_seq_Project/results/deseq2_salmon/tables/MG_vs_GC.csv")
res_a1g_salmon <- read.csv("/export/space3/users/zyanyava/2026-2/Transcriptomica/RNA_seq_Project/results/deseq2_salmon/tables/A1G_vs_GC.csv")

# Unir por gene_name
res_salmon <- merge(
  res_mg_salmon[, c("gene_name", "log2FoldChange", "padj")],
  res_a1g_salmon[, c("gene_name", "log2FoldChange", "padj")],
  by = "gene_name",
  suffixes = c("_MG", "_A1G")
)

# Calcular correlación
cor_salmon <- cor(res_salmon$log2FoldChange_MG, res_salmon$log2FoldChange_A1G, use = "complete.obs")

# Clasificar significancia
res_salmon <- res_salmon %>%
  mutate(significant = case_when(
    padj_MG < 0.05 & padj_A1G < 0.05 ~ "Both significant",
    padj_MG < 0.05 ~ "Only MG",
    padj_A1G < 0.05 ~ "Only A1G",
    TRUE ~ "Not significant"
  ))

# Scatter plot
ggplot(res_salmon, aes(x = log2FoldChange_MG, y = log2FoldChange_A1G, color = significant)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 0.8) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Both significant" = "red", 
                                "Only MG" = "blue", 
                                "Only A1G" = "green", 
                                "Not significant" = "gray")) +
  labs(x = "log2FC MG vs GC",
       y = "log2FC A1G vs GC",
       title = "Salmon: MG vs GC vs A1G vs GC",
       subtitle = paste0("Pearson r = ", round(cor_salmon, 3))) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

### A.6.3 Comparación STAR vs Salmon (MG vs GC)

Finalmente, se comparan directamente los log2 fold changes obtenidos con ambos pipelines para el contraste MG vs GC.

```{r fig-scatter-star-salmon, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="A13: Correlación de log2FC entre STAR y Salmon (MG vs GC).", out.width="60%", fig.align="center", message=FALSE}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Leer resultados
res_star <- read.csv("/export/space3/users/zyanyava/2026-2/Transcriptomica/RNA_seq_Project/results/deseq2/tables/MG_vs_GC.csv")
res_salmon <- read.csv("/export/space3/users/zyanyava/2026-2/Transcriptomica/RNA_seq_Project/results/deseq2_salmon/tables/MG_vs_GC.csv")

# Unir por gene_name
res_combined <- merge(
  res_star[, c("gene_name", "log2FoldChange", "padj")],
  res_salmon[, c("gene_name", "log2FoldChange", "padj")],
  by = "gene_name",
  suffixes = c("_STAR", "_Salmon")
)

# Calcular correlación
cor_val <- cor(res_combined$log2FoldChange_STAR, res_combined$log2FoldChange_Salmon, use = "complete.obs")

# Clasificar significancia
res_combined <- res_combined %>%
  mutate(significant = case_when(
    padj_STAR < 0.05 & padj_Salmon < 0.05 ~ "Both significant",
    padj_STAR < 0.05 ~ "Only STAR",
    padj_Salmon < 0.05 ~ "Only Salmon",
    TRUE ~ "Not significant"
  ))

# Scatter plot
ggplot(res_combined, aes(x = log2FoldChange_STAR, y = log2FoldChange_Salmon, color = significant)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 0.8) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "black") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Both significant" = "red", 
                                "Only STAR" = "blue", 
                                "Only Salmon" = "green", 
                                "Not significant" = "gray")) +
  labs(x = "log2FC STAR",
       y = "log2FC Salmon",
       title = "Comparación STAR vs Salmon (MG vs GC)",
       subtitle = paste0("Pearson r = ", round(cor_val, 3))) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

Se observó una alta correlación en ambos pipelines entre los contrastes MG vs GC y A1G vs GC, así como entre los propios pipelines, lo que confirma la robustez de los resultados.


## A.7 Análisis de enriquecimiento funcional (GSEA)

Figuras completas del análisis GSEA con WebGestalt.

```{r fig-gsea-barchart, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="A14: Top términos GO enriquecidos por GSEA (|NES| > 1.5).", out.width="70%", fig.align="center", warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
knitr::include_graphics("/export/space3/users/zyanyava/2026-2/Transcriptomica/RNA_seq_Project/results/GSEA/Project_MG_vs_GC/MG_vs_GC_barchart.png")
```

```{r fig-gsea-volcano, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="A15: Volcano plot del análisis GSEA.", out.width="60%", fig.align="center", warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
knitr::include_graphics("/export/space3/users/zyanyava/2026-2/Transcriptomica/RNA_seq_Project/results/GSEA/Project_MG_vs_GC/MG_vs_GC_enrichment__plot.png")
```

```{r fig-gsea-dag, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="A16: DAG (Directed Acyclic Graph) de términos GO enriquecidos.", out.width="90%", fig.align="center", warning=FALSE, message=FALSE }
knitr::include_graphics("/export/space3/users/zyanyava/2026-2/Transcriptomica/RNA_seq_Project/results/GSEA/Project_MG_vs_GC/MG_vs_GC_DAG.png")
```
